item.source:markdown:

item.title: Fired CHP officer, DUI suspect charged in freeway crash that killed four
item.description:

item.title: California passed a law to curb spikes in gas prices. Why isn’t it using those powers now?
item.description:
California built a first-in-the-nation system to police refinery profits during price spikes. Regulators delayed it for five years. Gasoline has surged to record highs as global oil markets spike amid the Iran war. The spike exposes a deeper problem: fewer refineries and limited California fuel supply.

The post California passed a law to curb spikes in gas prices. Why isn’t it using those powers now? appeared first on Long Beach Post News.
item.source:markdown:
![](https://img.lbpost.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/13095414/030926-socal-gas-prices-zc-cm-15-462727-gx0kafyw-639971-U9XadmiO-1024x682.webp)
California built a first-in-the-nation system to police refinery profits during price spikes. Regulators delayed it for five years. Gasoline has surged to record highs as global oil markets spike amid the Iran war. The spike exposes a deeper problem: fewer refineries and limited California fuel supply.
The post [California passed a law to curb spikes in gas prices. Why isn’t it using those powers now?](https://lbpost.com/news/california-passed-a-law-to-curb-spikes-in-gas-prices-why-isnt-it-using-those-powers-now) appeared first on [Long Beach Post News](https://lbpost.com/news).
